title: Phase 7: keepalive discovery and systematic recovery map
notes: Phase 7 closed with a strong mapping exit. Final ledger: 148 substantive entries, 72 fruitful / 76 exploration. Direct keepalive discovery still not robust. Medium-source forceoracle->memoryreadout is now a verified 5-seed recovery branch. Strong-source partial-init transfer cleanly separates route transfer from content recovery. Keepalive-anchor adapter ES remains the strongest verified ES result, while medium and teacher sources show a consistent adapter-vs-router-only tradeoff. Phase-8 follow-up filed for teacher-seeded direct keepalive basin entry.
close_reason: Phase-7 strong mapping exit complete; report, lessons, scorecards, ledger, verification, and follow-up issue filed.

title: Phase 3: explicit keyed packet memory for delayed retrieval
description: Design and test a content-memory intervention that stores trigger payloads explicitly rather than relying on the current delay-carry path. Prioritize keyed or multi-slot memory plus a retrieval-aware auxiliary objective.
notes: Release-stage control finished. Starting from the same oraclewarm payloadaux checkpoint, masked release collapses back to phase-2 behavior (test 0.4473, delay_rate 0.0039, early_exit_rate 0.9922), while explicit no-mask release keeps real delayed routing (test 0.6328, delay_rate 0.7027, route_match 0.5130). This strongly implicates the metadata-driven train-time masks as the main cause of the old Benchmark B v2 policy collapse.
close_reason: Implemented and validated explicit packet memory plus release-stage experiments. The intervention changed Benchmark B v2 from immediate-exit collapse to a reproducible hard-routing improvement when the train-time masks are removed at release, and the remaining bottleneck is now tracked separately.

title: Implement packet-routing benchmarks and core model
description: Build the synthetic benchmarks, packet-routing temporal graph model, routing semantics, and shared utilities.
notes: Completed packet-routing core model and both synthetic benchmarks. Added hard/soft routing modes, mailbox DELAY semantics, oracle-routing benchmark, and long-horizon memory benchmark with reproducible seeded sampling.
close_reason: Implemented the packet-routing model, synthetic benchmarks, routing semantics, and supporting benchmark utilities.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: source-logit content distillation on the 12514 frozen-1201 interpolation boundary
description: From e9w: the 12514 confirmation-aware interpolation recipe reran exactly and improved the base frozen-1201 tradeoff over 12423 (better overall, fq_acc, fq_route, and fq_exit), but phase11_verify shows its held-confirm metrics are effectively identical to 12423 across full_locked, finalquery_heavy, and longdistance. Next step: keep the frozen 1201 core and the 12514 interpolation head path fixed, and test content-only distillation from the strong source model logits/answers into that head to see whether the held-confirm content ceiling can move without sacrificing the improved base tradeoff.
close_reason: Source-logit distillation on the frozen-1201 12514 boundary was a rerun-backed negative: 12612 matched exactly and phase11_verify stayed identical to 12514 on held confirms while base tradeoff worsened.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: source-logit content distillation on the stable 13411 readout-only prefix boundary
description: The 2tl family established that narrow selector-only and very-light confirm-mix changes around the stable 13411 readout-only prefix boundary do not beat 13411 on the deciding base final-query tradeoff. Start from the same frozen 1201 core and the same 12713/13111 readout-only prefix blend as 13411, and test whether direct source-logit content distillation can improve content without giving back the imported held-confirm plateau. Keep the intervention narrow: no sink-prefix reopening, no full checkpoint blending, and no broad objective sweep outside the distillation line.
notes: Round 2 finished. 13613 (lw=0.15) selected step 383 with val 0.6636 / 0.3843 / 0.7766 / 123.41. 13614 (selectlocked_overall, lw=0.10) selected step 263 with val 0.6587 / 0.3803 / 0.7696 / 123.14. Neither beat 13612, so 13612 is the decisive winner and is moving to exact same-seed rerun now.
close_reason: Exact rerun matched 13612 at step 335, so the delayed-only 13111 source-logit scout gain is real. But phase11_verify showed the held-confirm plateau stayed identical to 13411 and confirmed base accuracy slipped slightly, so the family closes as a rerun-backed mixed result rather than a promotable improvement.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: confirm-aware selector/objective on the 13411 readout-only prefix boundary
description: Prefix-only 12713/13111 interpolation narrowed the transferable improvement to readout-only weights. 13411 (readout-only 95/5) exactly reran at step 215 and improved the frozen-1201 base frontier over both 12713 and 13211 while preserving the imported held-confirm plateau. Test whether a narrow confirm-aware selector/objective change from this readout-only boundary can move the held-confirm metrics without giving back the new base gain. Start with selector-only and very light confirm-mix variants; do not reopen sink prefixes or full-checkpoint blending.
notes: Round 2 finished. 13513 improved late and selected step 311 with val 0.6450/0.3525/0.7786/123.96, but 13511 remained the actual top variant at step 359 with val 0.6509/0.3635/0.7766/123.85. Neither beat 13411 on final-query accuracy, so the family still looks negative. Exact rerun of 13511 is now running from the same config with a dedicated rerun1 YAML.
close_reason: Exact rerun matched 13511 at step 359 and all four selector/objective variants stayed below the 13411 readout-only prefix boundary on base final-query accuracy, so the family is retired as a rerun-backed negative.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: prefix-only 12713/13111 interpolation on frozen-1201
description: Full-checkpoint interpolation between 12713 and 13111 is now mapped: 95/5 is the stable local optimum, and finer nearby weights only produce weaker points or reproducible transients. Test whether importing only the decisive reader prefixes from 13111 into the stronger 12713 boundary can keep the imported held-confirm plateau while recovering the remaining base gap. Start with readout_mlp / multiview_ / query_readout_proj style prefixes only; keep the frozen 1201 core unchanged.
notes: Prefix-only source-filtered interpolation is now narrowed to reader-side prefixes only. Original 13411 readout-only 95/5 selected best at step 215 and phase11_verify confirm gave base 0.6479/0.3826/0.7884/123.97 with held-confirm plateau retained at full_locked 0.1306/0.2539/0.9876/126.43. Sink-only 13412 retired early at step 23; sink+readout 13413 and readout-only 90/10 13415 both topped out at step 71 and did not justify promotion. Exact rerun 13411_rerun1 is live and has already reproduced the moving-best progression through steps 71 -> 143 -> 191.
close_reason: Prefix-only interpolation found a real frozen-1201 improvement localized to readout-only weights. 13411 exactly reran through the full selected-checkpoint progression and beat both 12713 and 13211 on the base tradeoff while preserving the imported held-confirm plateau; sink-only, sink+readout, and heavier readout branches were retired as noncompetitive.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: route-aware selector on probe-initialized frozen-1201 reader
description: Starting from 8te: selector-only rebalance on the probe-initialized two-view frozen-1201 reader improved overall and fq accuracy but gave back too much route. Weight-only and light confirm-mix variants were worse. Next test: keep the same frozen 1201 core and same probe-initialized two-view reader, but use an explicit route-aware selection rule or equivalent proxy so the 11914 route regime is not discarded while overall behavior improves.
notes: Ran four fair route-aware selector variants on the frozen 1201 probe-init reader. 12111 and 12112 collapsed into route-dead short-exit checkpoints. 12114 did the same. 12113 was the only late-exit branch but still landed at test overall=0.3346 fq_acc=0.2654 fq_route=0.3803 fq_exit=107.06 which is below both 11914 and 12011. Exact rerun matched. Retiring soft composite selection as a rerun-backed negative.
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 2 audit Benchmark B and diagnose collapse
description: Audit Benchmark B v1, add oracle/heuristic analyses, and determine whether failure comes from benchmark design, objective incentives, or model/search limits.
notes: Audit complete enough to guide the second wave: Benchmark B v1 is a true delayed-memory task with a single oracle route and break-even CE that still favors delaying, but even near-perfect delayed routing stays at chance. Benchmark B v2 was added because v1 does not test adaptive routing; v2 makes delay objectively plausible and exposes validation collapse into easy-exit rather than successful delayed retrieval.
close_reason: Benchmark B audit completed. v1 was preserved as a fixed-route memory stress test, v2 was added as the adaptive-routing benchmark, and the collapse was localized to memory/generalization rather than reward impossibility.

title: Phase 6: controller discovery and staged recovery map
notes: Phase-6 update: the last strong-basin isolation reruns finished and both matched exactly. keepalive->memoryreadout_nosink reproduced at overall 0.9596 / route 0.9258 / fq 0.9793 / fq route 0.9706 / fq exit 125.92. keepalive->memoryonly reproduced at overall 0.9613 / route 0.9323 / fq 0.9826 / fq route 0.9739 / fq exit 126.13. With verification on the p1 runs, the reopening threshold is now tight: in the strong keepalive basin, reopened memory alone is already sufficient for near-perfect long-horizon recovery; readout and sink_proj are optional refinements; packet_adapter is not required. This closes the current recovery-ablation slice at a clean stopping point.

title: Phase 2 implement Benchmark B interventions and reruns
description: Implement at least three intervention families for Benchmark B collapse, run dev/main sweeps, and promote the best candidate to final seed comparisons.
notes: The completed dev hybrid write-aux pop64 run does not rescue delayed retrieval: train generations reached delay_rate around 0.7, delay_write_mean around 0.2-0.3, and route_match 1.0, but final test still collapsed to early_exit_rate 1.0 with delay_rate 0.0. Promoted T=256 hybrid main rerun on the stable mask-curriculum path shows the same collapse by the first validation checkpoint.
close_reason: Completed architecture, curriculum, and hybrid-ES intervention families; ran dev sweeps, promoted h256 reruns, and final 3-seed comparison. Conclusion is a strong negative on delayed-memory generalization with clear next bottleneck.

title: Phase 8: teacher-seeded direct keepalive basin entry
description: Use the verified route-faithful adapter-ES checkpoint as a fixed teacher for a direct keepalive student, distilling only wait/release/control-state channels before releasing into task-only hard routing. Goal: turn the phase-7 route-faithful teacher regime into a robust from-scratch discovery basin.
notes: Phase 8 closed as a strong mapping result. Strong keepalive-teacher wait/release-only supervision plus long release and delayed dropout created a real teacher-free route-faithful basin-entry effect across multiple seeds, but five-seed content quality stayed weak. The sharpest new boundary is that reopening memory destabilizes fragile teacher-free basins, while head-only reopenings preserve route but do not solve content. Follow-up filed as gnn2-vi6.
close_reason: Phase 8 campaign completed locally with docs, ledger, verification, and next-step issue filed as gnn2-vi6.

title: Phase 2 reporting, lessons, and handoff
description: Write phase2_plan, phase2_report, phase2_lessons, update README commands, and land/push all results with a clear next-step recommendation.
notes: README phase-2 commands and docs/phase2_lessons.md are in place. Final report regeneration and handoff will follow after the promoted h256 run and the seed sweep complete.
close_reason: Phase 2 plan, report, lessons, README commands, follow-up issues, and final handoff are complete. Remaining work is tracked in new phase-3 issues.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: held-confirm proxy selection on frozen 1874 baseline
description: Start from the frozen 1874 multiview query-gated baseline and the negative result from gnn2-e7s. Do not add broader reader changes or memory touches. Test narrower held-confirm proxy alignment moves such as selection-metric changes, confirm-proxy early stopping, or consistency penalties that explicitly target the strong-source held-confirm ceiling without changing routing or the source family.
notes: Implemented training-time selection proxy eval support for frozen-reader runs. Added selection_eval_benchmarks loader, validation-time proxy scoring, phase11 selection sweep configs for full_locked/finalqueryheavy/longdistance selectors, and tests. Code gate: 52 passed in 9.61s.
close_reason: Completed four proxy-selection variants on the frozen 1874 baseline, confirmed the best selector, and exact-reran it. Selecting by full_locked final-query accuracy was the strongest proxy, but held-confirm metrics remained at the same ceiling as earlier strong-source baselines, so the family is retired as a reproducible negative. See docs/phase11_71v_notes.md and docs/phase11_run_matrix.csv.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: interpolate frozen-1201 checkpoints 12713 and 13111
description: 13111 is the best very-light delayed-only source-distill point on frozen 1201: it slightly improves base route/exit versus 12713 but still trails 12713 on overall and final-query accuracy, while held-confirm metrics remain on the same plateau. Test same-architecture checkpoint/head interpolation between the stronger base boundary 12713 and the better-route delayed-distill point 13111 to see whether those strengths can be combined without changing the frozen 1201 core.
close_reason: 95/5 interpolation between 12713 and 13111 reran exactly and imported the 13111 held-confirm plateau, but it still did not beat 12713 on base overall or final-query accuracy.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: 1874 content distillation onto frozen-1201 MLP boundary 12713
description: From gnn2-j61: cross-source query-gated head geometry did not rescue frozen-1201 portability. Direct 11011 transfers and softer 11011/11811 blends all stayed near-route-dead. Next step: avoid the failed query-gated portability path entirely and test whether stronger frozen-1874 content can transfer onto the best frozen-1201 MLP boundary 12713 via content-only distillation or supervision, while preserving the 12713 route structure.
close_reason: Stronger 1874 source-logit distillation onto frozen-1201 boundary 12713 mapped cleanly: final-query-only branches were noncompetitive, while delayed-only distillation (12914) reran exactly and slightly improved held-confirm metrics, but it still lost base overall/final-query accuracy versus 12713 so the family closes as a rerun-backed mixed result rather than a frontier win.

title: Run comparisons and write experiment report
description: Run smoke tests and at least one meaningful comparison suite, then produce the final markdown report with honest conclusions.
notes: Completed a dev comparison suite with Benchmark A method comparisons and ES ablations, plus Benchmark B horizon scaling runs and a generated markdown report with plots/tables.
close_reason: Ran the dev comparison suite and ablations, generated plots/tables, and wrote the final markdown experiment report.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: objective rebalance on probe-initialized frozen-1201 two-view reader
description: Starting from by8: the two-view probe-initialized frozen-1201 reader recovered a real final-query regime and reran exactly, but at an unacceptable overall-cost tradeoff. 11914 slightly beat 11732 on fq_acc and fq_route while collapsing overall accuracy and exiting earlier. Next test: keep the strong 1201 core frozen and keep the probe-initialized decisive-view reader, but rebalance the objective and selector so the recovered final-query regime is preserved without destroying non-final-query behavior.
notes: Finished four-way objective-rebalance sweep plus exact rerun of the best scout. 12011 selector-only rebalance reran exactly at test overall=0.5264 fq_acc=0.3189 fq_route=0.3964 fq_exit=122.12. 12012 weight-only rebalance collapsed into a wrong basin with test overall=0.6217 fq_acc=0.8997 fq_route=0.0074 fq_exit=126.60. 12013 combined selector plus weight stayed near the selector-only tradeoff at 0.5293 / 0.2627 / 0.3730 / 117.98. 12014 light confirm-mix was actively harmful at 0.5505 / 0.3155 / 0.0000 / 25.73. Notes in docs/phase11_8te_notes.md and ledger rows appended in docs/phase11_run_matrix.csv. Conclusion: objective rebalance alone cannot preserve the 11914 route regime while repairing overall behavior.
close_reason: Objective rebalance on the probe-initialized frozen-1201 reader is a rerun-backed negative: selector-only rebalance helps overall behavior but gives back too much route, and stronger weighting or light confirm-mix are worse.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: very-light delayed-only 1874 distillation on frozen-1201
description: 13011 showed that reducing delayed-only logits distillation from 0.50 to 0.25 recovers some base behavior while leaving held-confirm metrics unchanged relative to 12914. Run an even lighter delayed-only sweep around 0.10-0.20 on the same frozen-1201 12713 boundary to test whether the remaining base gap to 12713 can be closed while preserving that held-confirm plateau.
close_reason: Very-light delayed-only 1874 distillation produced a rerun-backed mixed result: 13111 was the best balanced point but did not beat 12713 and did not move the held-confirm plateau.

title: Phase 5: multi-seed ES polish from working final-query checkpoints
description: Phase 4 found that scratch hybrid ES still collapses during warmstart on the improved architecture, but resume-based hybrid ES from the best phase4 hard-ST checkpoint reached perfect Benchmark B v2 performance on the same benchmark configuration. Follow up with a proper multi-seed panel that resumes from working hard-ST checkpoints, compares ES polish against additional gradient-only continuation, and measures whether ES local search is a reliable refinement stage even if it is not a robust discovery mechanism from scratch.
notes: Weak-basin role-map update: adapter+ES from seed947 achieves perfect route timing but only partial task recovery (confirm overall 0.9131, final-query acc 0.8276). Router-only ES from the same weak checkpoint is worse (confirm overall 0.8320, final-query acc 0.6668) while keeping route_match 1.0. Current interpretation: medium-basin rescue is controller-only, weak-basin rescue is limited by content quality and benefits from adapters.

title: Phase 3b: long-distance final-query retrieval after no-mask release
description: Target the remaining Benchmark B v2 bottleneck after the successful no-mask release result. The model now sustains real DELAY behavior and largely solves delay_to_trigger_exit, but delay_to_final_query remains near chance even with heavy delay usage and explicit packet memory. Focus next on longer-range retention/retrieval and query-conditioned decoding rather than on route willingness alone.
notes: Phase 4 complete. Scratch hybrid ES on the improved architecture still collapsed during warmstart (final-query acc ~0.24-0.25, route_match 0, exit_time 0), but timeout fix landed and a resume-based hybrid ES retest from the best sticky_aux_router2 hard-ST checkpoint was successful. Direct reevaluation of the resumed hard-ST checkpoint under the current eval loop gave overall 0.801 / final-query 0.593 / exit_time 77.34 / route_match 0.448. Resume-based hybrid ES on the same Benchmark B v2 configuration improved that single-seed checkpoint to overall 1.000 / final-query 1.000 / exit_time 127 / route_match 1.000 on 2 GPUs. Primary conclusion remains a strong negative on robust controller discovery across seeds; secondary conclusion is that ES polish looks promising once a working controller checkpoint already exists.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add tests, configs, scripts, and reporting pipeline
description: Add reproducible configs, smoke/main scripts, unit tests, profiling, plotting, and report generation.
notes: Resumed after compaction. Confirmed missing deliverables are tests, run scripts, README expansion, and report generation. Current code and configs exist; next step is infrastructure completion plus any training/eval fixes needed for reporting.
close_reason: Added tests, configs, run scripts, report generation, README updates, and reproducible smoke/dev execution paths.

title: Phase 2 add routing and ES diagnostics
description: Add route-action, mailbox, TTL, confidence, reward-variance, and failure-slice diagnostics plus report plots for Benchmark B.
notes: Added phase-2 routing diagnostics, benchmark audit CLI, slice/per-mode metrics, mailbox/TTL/confidence stats, ES reward variance logging, and a phase2 report generator with plots under docs/phase2_assets/.
close_reason: Phase 2 diagnostics landed: route-action, transition, mailbox, TTL, confidence, slice, per-mode, and ES reward diagnostics plus updated report plots.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: tune around the 12412 frozen-1201 interpolation boundary
description: From y4x: interpolating the balanced 12011 head with the routeful 11914 head on the same frozen 1201 core produced the first clearly better tradeoff. The 50/50 overall-selected blend 12412 reran exactly at overall=0.6276 fq_acc=0.3295 fq_route=0.7527 fq_exit=124.26, beating 11732, 11914, and 12011 simultaneously. Next step: stay on the same frozen 1201 core and same probe-view reader, and locally tune around this new boundary with nearby interpolation weights and/or selection rules rather than reverting to unrelated reader families.
close_reason: Local tuning around the 12412 interpolation boundary found a stable better point. The 45/55 balanced/routeful blend 12423 reached overall=0.6344 fq_acc=0.3215 fq_route=0.7674 fq_exit=123.75 and reran exactly, improving overall and route over 12412 with only a small final-query accuracy giveback. The family is therefore closed as a rerun-backed positive refinement and ready for promotion/confirmation rather than more blind local sweeps.

title: Bootstrap repository and Python environment
description: Initialize the gnn2 repository with uv, Python 3.12, PyTorch nightly cu130, task tracking, and agent preference notes.
notes: Resolved PyTorch nightly installation by pinning torch/torchvision/torchaudio to the CUDA 13.0 nightly index and triton to the base nightly index. Verified torch 2.12.0.dev20260317+cu130 with CUDA 13.0.
close_reason: Bootstrapped the repo with uv, Python 3.12, PyTorch nightly cu130, lockfile, task tracking, and persistent AGENTS preferences.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: rebalance delayed-only 1874 distillation on frozen-1201
description: 12914 is the only 8bn branch that moved anything real: exact rerun matched and verify showed slightly stronger held-confirm metrics than 12713, but base overall/fq_acc got worse. Run a narrow rebalance sweep around 12914 to see whether a lighter delayed-only weight or delayed-only-plus-light-finalquery mix can keep the small held-confirm gain while recovering the base tradeoff on frozen 1201.
close_reason: Delayed-only 1874 distillation on frozen-1201 was rebalanced fairly: lighter weight 13011 reran exactly and improved base behavior versus 12914 while keeping the same held-confirm metrics, but it still did not beat the 12713 base frontier; selector changes were weaker, so the family closes as a rerun-backed mixed result.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: probe-initialized frozen-1201 reader from decisive views
description: Starting from t2d and o8g: the exact frozen 1201 anchor is perfectly decodable at sink/readout views, but both linear and query-conditioned online readers fail to realize that decodability. Next test: use the decisive frozen views only and initialize or supervise the online reader from the offline probe family that already solves the frozen decoding problem. Goal: determine whether the remaining gap is mainly optimization/init rather than reader expressivity.
notes: Finished four-way decisive-view sweep plus exact rerun. Result: probe warmstart materially changed frozen-1201 dynamics. 11912 moved one-view MLP from near-immediate exit to late exits but route stayed weak. 11914 with final_sink_state plus sink_state_query and probe warmstart matched and slightly exceeded 11732 on final-query accuracy and route at test overall=0.2598 fq_acc=0.3015 fq_route=0.6317 fq_exit=107.91 and rerun1 matched exactly. Family is closed as a rerun-backed negative because it still destroys overall behavior relative to 11732 overall=0.5238 fq_acc=0.2620 fq_route=0.6197 fq_exit=120.08. Notes in docs/phase11_by8_notes.md and ledger rows appended in docs/phase11_run_matrix.csv.
close_reason: Probe-initialized decisive-view frozen-1201 readers are rerun-backed negatives: warmstart helps a lot and 11914 recovers 11732-level final-query behavior, but the tradeoff is still far too destructive overall.

title: Phase 11: confirmation-aware objective on frozen 1874 baseline
description: Start from the best confirmed strong-source frozen reader on teacher-free source 1874 and test confirmation-aware training rather than broader reader search. Keep memory/router/control frozen by default. Prioritize explicit confirmation-mixed schedules, held-confirm weighting, or consistency-style objectives on the existing strong frozen baseline. Compare directly against the phase-9 query-gated finalqweight family and the strongest phase-10 multiview family. Avoid broad new reader families, fragile 1879 tuning, or broad memory touches unless the new objective clearly justifies them.
notes: Phase 11 objective-mix scouts 11011/11012 finished cleanly from the frozen 1874 baseline. Summary metrics: 11011 overall=0.9907 fq_acc=0.9811 fq_route=0.9424 fq_exit=121.46; 11012 overall=0.9897 fq_acc=0.9791 fq_route=0.9404 fq_exit=121.14. Launched next pair 11013/11014 to test finalqueryheavy and longdistance mix variants while preserving frozen memory/router/control.
close_reason: Completed four confirmation-aware objective-mix variants on frozen 1874, confirmed the two best candidates, and exact-reran both lead branches. Validation improved modestly but held-confirm metrics did not beat the established strong-source ceiling, so the family is retired as a reproducible negative. See docs/phase11_e7s_notes.md and docs/phase11_run_matrix.csv.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: confirmation-aware objective tuning from the 12423 frozen-1201 interpolation optimum
description: From udf: the 45/55 interpolation optimum 12423 reran exactly and improves base behavior over 12412, but phase11_verify confirms show the held-confirm metrics are effectively identical to 12412 across full_locked, finalquery_heavy, and longdistance. Next step: start from the frozen 1201 12423 interpolation optimum and change the objective and/or selector in a confirmation-aware way, rather than doing more interpolation weight sweeps.
close_reason: 12514 reran exactly and improved the frozen-1201 base tradeoff over 12423, but phase11_verify confirms stayed identical to 12423 across full_locked, finalquery_heavy, and longdistance. Branch closed as a rerun-backed positive on base behavior and a strong mapping result on held-confirm limits.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: route-preserving training-time regularization on frozen-1201 probe-init reader
description: From gds: soft composite selectors, pure lexicographic selectors, and hard route-floor selectors all failed on the frozen 1201 probe-initialized two-view reader. The least-bad floor variant 12213 reran exactly at overall=0.4323 fq_acc=0.2694 fq_route=0.2647 fq_exit=84.75, and no validation checkpoint ever satisfied a meaningful held-confirm route floor. Next step: keep the same frozen 1201 core and probe-init reader, but move beyond selector-only changes into light training-time route-preserving regularization or rollback logic that explicitly discourages collapse of held-confirm final-query route match during optimization.
close_reason: Exact rerun of the least-bad delayed-only route-action regularizer matched at overall=0.5312 fq_acc=0.2988 fq_route=0.0321 fq_exit=80.95; all four light proxy-teacher route-preserving regularizers stayed far below both the useful route boundary and the better-balanced selector baseline, so the family is retired as a stable negative.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: lexicographic route-floor selector on frozen-1201 probe-init reader
description: From 4nm: soft weighted-sum and weighted-geomean selectors on the frozen 1201 probe-initialized two-view reader all failed. Three selectors collapsed into route-dead short-exit checkpoints and the only late-exit branch 12113 reran exactly at overall=0.3346 fq_acc=0.2654 fq_route=0.3803 fq_exit=107.06 which is below both 11914 and 12011. Next step: keep the same frozen 1201 core and same probe-init reader but replace soft composite selection with a hard route floor or lexicographic selector so routeful checkpoints cannot be traded away for overall fit too early.
notes: Lexicographic and hard route-floor selector sweep completed on frozen 1201 probe-init reader. 12211 pure route->overall collapsed to overall=0.2347 fq_acc=0.3115 fq_route=0.0000 fq_exit=21.75. 12212 route->fq_acc->overall stayed late-exit but weak at 0.3079 / 0.2841 / 0.1237 / 105.27. 12213 route-floor>=0.50 was least-bad at 0.4323 / 0.2694 / 0.2647 / 84.75 and reran exactly. 12214 route-floor>=0.55 never hit its floor and landed at 0.4323 / 0.2794 / 0.0922 / 59.93. Conclusion: selector-only fixes are exhausted; no checkpoint ever satisfied a meaningful held-confirm route floor.
close_reason: Closed

title: Import EGGROLL paper and reference code
description: Download the original EGGROLL paper, convert it to markdown, clone the HyperscaleES reference repo, compare the two, and record mandatory reference instructions in AGENTS.md.
notes: Downloaded the original EGGROLL paper and cloned HyperscaleES. Replaced the raw markdown conversion with a cleaner text-layer extraction in references/eggroll_paper.md, kept the old converter output as references/eggroll_paper_pymupdf4llm_raw.md, wrote docs/eggroll_reference_alignment.md, and updated AGENTS.md with mandatory EGGROLL reference discipline for later PyTorch work.
close_reason: Original EGGROLL paper and reference repo imported, cleaned, compared, and encoded into repo instructions.

title: Phase 5: stabilize final-query wait controller across seeds
description: Follow up on phase 4 strong-negative result. Phase 4 showed that explicit sticky control can produce real single-seed improvements on Benchmark B v2 delay_to_final_query, but promoted seed panels collapsed back to premature exit with zero route match. The next campaign should target controller robustness rather than basic memory capacity: factorized wait/not-wait controller, stronger temporal consistency on the wait latch, per-mode balancing, or direct route-head restructuring so the decodable control state reliably governs the hard router across seeds.
notes: Continuation update: hard-ST direct release-gate variants still failed to discover durable final-query behavior. Full-benchmark releasegate seeds 940/941 only pushed final-query exit to ~24.45 / ~33.87 with fq acc ~0.2265 / ~0.2570 and near-zero route_match. The finalquery-only direct-gate control run (seed952) was retired at step 99 after val stayed at chance (~0.2496) with fq route_match 0 and fq exit ~19.49. Current interpretation: hard-ST discovery remains the open problem; current ES results are stronger than the hard-ST fixes.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: cross-source head interpolation from strong frozen-1874 into frozen-1201
description: From gnn2-kwy: same-source head blending on frozen-1201 found a real base-tradeoff improvement in 12713, but held-confirms stayed effectively identical to 12514. Next step: keep the frozen 1201 core and the 12713 objective/selector fixed, but blend in head geometry from the stronger frozen-1874 boundary to test whether held-confirm content can transfer across decodable sources rather than only within the 1201 family.
close_reason: Cross-source 1874-to-1201 query-gated head transfer was a clean four-variant negative: direct transfers and softer 11011/11811 blends all stayed near-route-dead on validation under the 12514 selector discipline.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: multiview-fusion-only adapter on frozen 1874 baseline
description: Start from the same frozen 1874 multiview query-gated selector baseline after the negatives from objective mixing, proxy regularizers, focal/margin shaping, prototype heads, and mixture-of-heads readers. Keep routing, memory, control, and the answer head simple, but allow a tiny route-preserving adapter only on the multiview fusion path before the final head. The goal is to test whether the remaining held-confirm gap comes from needing a small amount of read-path adaptation rather than more head geometry or specialization.
notes: Completed the multiview-fusion-only adapter follow-up on frozen 1874. Tried four fair variants: affine fusion-only (11711), low-rank-r4 fusion-only (11712), affine+qproj (11713), and low-rank-r4+qproj (11714). All four were dramatically below the selector-only 11101 baseline on validation summary metrics. Best was 11714 at overall 0.4971 / fq_acc 0.5174 / fq_route 0.9315 / fq_exit 120.52, and 11714_rerun1 matched exactly. Family is retired as a rerun-backed negative; tiny fusion-path touches are not enough.
close_reason: Completed: multiview-fusion-only adapters on frozen 1874 are a rerun-backed negative.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: frozen-1201 head-init and blend search beyond 12514
description: From gnn2-28d: source-logit content distillation on the frozen-1201 12514 boundary was a rerun-backed negative. 12612 matched exactly, verify confirms were identical to 12514 on full_locked/finalquery_heavy/longdistance, and the whole family weakened the base tradeoff. Next step: keep the frozen 1201 core and the 12514 objective/selector fixed, but change how the head path is initialized or blended so we test whether a better frozen-1201 boundary comes from head geometry rather than another teacher loss.
close_reason: Head blending on frozen-1201 beyond 12514 was rerun-backed positive on the base tradeoff: 12713 matched exactly and improved base overall/fq_acc/fq_route over 12514, but phase11_verify showed held-confirms stayed effectively unchanged.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: mixture-of-heads answer reader on frozen 1874 baseline
description: Start from the same frozen 1874 multiview query-gated selector baseline after the negatives from objective mixing, proxy regularizers, focal/margin shaping, and prototype heads. Keep routing, memory, control, and the frozen multiview reader path fixed, but replace the single global answer head with a tiny mixture-of-heads or branch-specialized answer reader. The goal is to test whether the remaining held-confirm gap comes from needing multiple specialized answer subspaces rather than one shared linear or metric geometry.
notes: Mixture-of-heads sweep complete on frozen 1874. Variants 11611-11614 all stayed below the selector-only baseline 11101. Best variant 11612 (4 input-gated heads) reached overall 0.9868 / fq_acc 0.9732 / fq_route 0.9414 / fq_exit 121.37, and 11612_rerun1 matched exactly. Query-conditioned gating and light balance regularization did not improve the tradeoff, so the family is now a reproducible negative.
close_reason: Mixture-of-heads readers on frozen 1874 are a rerun-backed negative. The best variant improved over the other mixtures but still stayed clearly below 11101 on route and exit, and the rerun matched exactly.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: proxy-agreement regularizer on frozen 1874 baseline
description: Start from the same frozen 1874 multiview query-gated baseline used in gnn2-71v and gnn2-zpw. Do not change the reader, routing, memory, or controller. Test a narrower objective-alignment follow-up that regularizes the main training objective against confirm-like proxy predictions or logits, rather than interpolating extra proxy CE losses. Focus on agreement-style penalties, trust-region style KL penalties, or similarly tight proxy-consistency terms that keep the optimization anchored to the strong base reader while explicitly discouraging held-confirm drift.
notes: Implemented a frozen-source proxy-agreement path in run.py using logits-only teacher KL on auxiliary confirm-like batches. Launched initial 1874 scouts: 11311 full_locked@0.05 and 11312 full_locked@0.1, both with full_locked fq-accuracy selection and final_query_only agreement scope.
close_reason: Completed as a reproducible negative: 4 proxy-agreement KL variants on frozen 1874 all underperformed the selector-only baseline on validation, and the lead mixed variant 11313 matched exactly on rerun. Proxy-agreement regularization did not move the held-confirm ceiling.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: fine interpolation near frozen-1201 boundary 12713
description: 13211 showed that a 95/5 checkpoint blend of 12713 and 13111 can import the 13111 held-confirm plateau while staying close to the 12713 base regime, but it still trails 12713 slightly on base overall and final-query accuracy. Sweep finer interpolation points nearer 12713 (for example 97.5/2.5, 96/4, 94/6) to test whether the remaining base gap can be closed while preserving the imported confirm plateau.
close_reason: Fine interpolation near 12713 did not beat the stable 95/5 point; 96/4 reran exactly but the gain was transient and decayed the same way.

title: Phase 5 follow-up: content refinement after ES route rescue
description: Use the weak-basin phase-5 result as the starting point for a narrower follow-up. Resume from a weak hard-ST checkpoint such as results/phase5_dev/hard_st_b_v2_control_router2_setclear_oraclecontrol_opt_seed947_p1, use ES to recover perfect final-query routing, then freeze routing and run a short gradient-only content/readout refinement phase. The target is to lift final-query accuracy above the current ~0.83 weak-basin ES plateau without losing the recovered route timing.

title: Implement training methods and distributed low-rank ES
description: Add soft-routing, hard straight-through, and hybrid EGGROLL-inspired low-rank ES training with 2-GPU population evaluation.
notes: Implemented supervised soft and hard-ST training plus distributed 2-GPU low-rank hybrid ES with antithetic seed-reconstructed perturbations, warm-start support, checkpointing, and profiling outputs.
close_reason: Implemented soft and hard-ST baselines plus 2-GPU hybrid low-rank ES training with warm-start, checkpointing, and profiling.

title: Download arXiv paper 2603.12228 and convert to markdown
description: After repo setup is complete, download https://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.12228, convert it to markdown, and manually clean up the markdown output.
notes: Downloaded arXiv PDF 2603.12228, converted it to markdown, removed pagination and OCR debris, and manually repaired the title block, small tables, and obvious text artifacts.
close_reason: Downloaded arXiv paper 2603.12228, converted it to markdown, and cleaned the markdown by hand.

title: Investigate rank-1 instability in long warmstart 2-GPU hybrid h256 run
description: Reproduce and diagnose the late warmstart rank-1 failure seen in the original configs/phase2/main/hybrid_es_benchmark_b_v2_maskcurr_h256.yaml run. Determine whether the issue is distributed runtime, PyTorch nightly instability, or a model-side bug.
notes: The long h256 hybrid main attempt crashed on rank 1 after repeated collapsed validation checkpoints; a shorter stability-adjusted rerun completed and confirmed the same scientific conclusion.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: source-native query-conditioned frozen-1201 reader
description: Starting from qiz/t2d/rnd: the exact 1201 anchor is perfectly decodable at sink/readout views, direct 1874-style reader ports collapse completely, and source-native linear heads improve over those broken ports but remain weak. Next test: keep the 1201 core frozen and train a source-native query-conditioned reader that only consumes the decisive sink/readout-side views, without inheriting 1874 geometry. Goal: determine whether 1201 can support a viable frozen query-aware reader when the interface matches the source family.
notes: Ran four source-native query-conditioned frozen-1201 readers using only sink_state_query and baseline_readout_input. 11811 native-sink query-gated peaked at overall=0.4819 fq_acc=0.2840 fq_route=0.0606. 11812 native-sink FiLM peaked at 0.4507 / 0.2483 / 0.0804. 11814 fresh-sink FiLM fit overall slightly better at 0.5010 / 0.2701 / 0.0000 but stayed route-dead. 11813 fresh-sink query-gated remained route-dead through step 239 and was stopped early. Net: query-conditioned source-native readers do not solve the frozen-1201 portability gap; none beat the simpler 11732 linear baseline.
close_reason: Retired as a reproducible portability negative: source-native query-conditioned frozen-1201 readers remained route-dead or near-route-dead and did not beat 11732.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: prototype answer head on frozen 1874 baseline
description: Start from the frozen 1874 multiview query-gated selector baseline after the negatives from e7s, 71v, zpw, m5v, and vok. Keep routing, memory, control, and the frozen reader path fixed, but replace the standard linear answer head with a prototype or metric-style answer head: normalized answer prototypes, cosine classifier, or small contrastive answer embedding objective. The goal is to test whether the remaining held-confirm gap is output-geometry-limited rather than objective-weight-limited.
notes: Prototype-head sweep complete on frozen 1874. Cosine/prototype variants 11511-11514 all improved validation answer fit slightly but weakened route and exit relative to the selector-only baseline 11101. Best variant 11514 (d64 + pull 0.1) reached overall 0.9951 / fq_acc 0.9911 / fq_route 0.9374 / fq_exit 121.05, and 11514_rerun1 matched exactly. Family is now a reproducible negative rather than a one-seed miss.
close_reason: Prototype / metric answer heads on frozen 1874 are a rerun-backed negative. The best variant improved plain validation answer fit slightly but consistently weakened route and exit relative to 11101, and the rerun matched exactly.

title: Phase 10: route-regularized read-path adapter on decodable 1874
description: Start from the best confirmed phase-9 strong-source frozen-head baseline on teacher-free source 1874 and test a single tightly constrained read-path adapter with explicit route-drift regularization. Goal: improve held-confirm content without reopening broad memory or destabilizing late-route fidelity. Use the phase-9 locked-confirm suite as the primary guardrail and compare directly against the frozen query-gated finalqweight family and the query-FILM family.
notes: Phase 10 completed locally. Strong-source decodable frozen readers and route-preserving read-path adapters were mapped fairly across 84 substantive runs (53 fruitful / 31 exploration). Main result: final_sink_state is the decisive frozen view; multiview and iterative readers improve base fit but not held-confirm content; the query-FILM low-rank adapter 10024 reproduced exactly as a single seed but failed its five-seed panel; portability to 1821/1842 improves base behavior but not held confirms; 1879 remains a negative control; head-level ES is a fair negative. Final local artifacts: docs/phase10_report.md, docs/phase10_lessons.md, docs/phase10_cluster_scorecards.md, docs/phase10_run_matrix.csv, docs/phase10_handoff.md.
close_reason: Phase 10 completed as a strong mapping exit; next step narrowed to a confirmation-aware objective on the best frozen 1874 baseline.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: Phase 10 anchors updated locally: 9142 and 9132 reproduced with phase10_verify confirms in the expected regime; 9111 and 9102 confirm jobs are running on CPU. First real scouts are now live on both GPUs in dedicated run dirs after fixing a shared-results-dir launch mistake: strong-source multiview sink+packet+baseline query-gated fusion (seed10012) on GPU0 and strong-source low-rank read-path adapter (seed10022) on GPU1. Ledger updated in docs/phase10_run_matrix.csv.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: Phase 10 update: 10043 (ES-anchor content distill) and 10052 (iterative cross-attn reader) both independently verified in the same held-confirm regime as the earlier 1874 multiview readers: strong base gains but full_locked final-query accuracy still about 0.298-0.299 at route~0.88 and exit~116. Affine (10021) and residual (10023) read-path adapters completed on 1874 and their confirm jobs are now running. Missing ES upper-bound anchor reproduction 1201 is also running locally on 2 GPUs; ledger updated in docs/phase10_run_matrix.csv.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: Phase 10 now has the strong-source verification block running instead of more single-seed scouts. The missing 1201 ES upper-bound anchor is reproduced and verified. Strong-source adapters (10021/10023), single-view ablations (10015/10016), two-view concat (10011), one-shot cross-attention (10014), and view-drop regularization (10044) all preserved route but stayed at the same held-confirm ceiling near fq_acc~0.296-0.309 on 1874. Portability checks on 1842 (10142/10143) and 1821 (10152/10132) improved base behavior but did not materially improve held confirms. A real 5-seed panel is now running for the strongest current 1874 multiview query-gated family: seeds 10212-10215 alongside existing seed10012.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: Phase 10 update: 1874 query-FILM 5-seed panel is now verified and lands on the same held-confirm ceiling as multiview query-gated. Means: base 0.8237 / 0.6392 / 0.9525 / 122.68; full_locked 0.6620 / 0.3060 / 0.8797 / 116.14; finalquery_heavy 0.4579 / 0.3164 / 0.8785 / 115.97; longdistance 0.5374 / 0.3463 / 0.8799 / 145.07. Cross-attn 5-seed panel also verifies to the same ceiling: base 0.9809 / 0.9711 / 0.9499 / 122.19; full_locked 0.6587 / 0.2991 / 0.8797 / 116.14. Current live work: 1879 negative-control 5-seed panel round 1 on GPU0, 1874 iterative cross-attn iter2 5-seed panel round 1 on GPU1. Added configs/scripts for iterative cluster and head-level ES cluster to close the remaining phase-10 coverage gaps.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: Phase 10 update: sink-only 1874 panel is confirmed. Five-seed means: base 0.8931 / 0.7811 / 0.9398 / 121.66; full_locked 0.6582 / 0.2981 / 0.8797 / 116.14; finalquery_heavy 0.4504 / 0.3069 / 0.8785 / 115.97; longdistance 0.5320 / 0.3387 / 0.8799 / 145.07. This sharpens the view map: final_sink_state alone is enough to reach the same held-confirm ceiling, while extra frozen views mostly buy base-fit rather than better held-confirm content. Iterative scout sweep is now running on the GPUs with the fixed result-dir logic.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: port selector baseline to frozen 1201 upper-bound source
description: Start from the strong ES-assisted 1201 source and port the best frozen selector baseline used on 1874. Goal: test whether the held-confirm ceiling is mainly source-quality-limited or reader/objective-limited by measuring the same frozen-reader recipe on a stronger decodable source. Keep routing/memory/control frozen by default and reuse the 11101-style selection rule before any fair variant work.
notes: Finished three frozen 1201 selector ports. Direct multiview 11721 collapsed at overall 0.4810 / fq_acc 0.2661 / fq_route 0.0000 / fq_exit 11.61. Simpler readout-only 11722 was least bad at 0.4868 / 0.2522 / 0.0000 / 29.75 and exact rerun 11722_rerun1 matched exactly. Query-gated 11723 also collapsed at 0.4771 / 0.2473 / 0.0000 / 19.84. Conclusion: stronger 1201 source quality alone does not make the frozen 1874 selector recipe portable; retire as rerun-backed negative and follow with a 1201 decodability/interface audit.
close_reason: Three frozen 1201 selector ports were executed and the least-bad readout-only port reran exactly. All variants collapsed into the immediate-exit basin with fq_route 0.0, so stronger 1201 source quality alone does not make the frozen 1874 selector recipe portable.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: zero-init frozen-1201 readout without 1874 port geometry
description: Keep the strong 1201 core frozen but stop porting the 1874 read-path parameterization. Train a zero-init or source-native readout-only head on top of the decodable 1201 sink and readout views, using the same selector discipline as qiz. Goal: test whether 1201 can support a compatible frozen readout when the interface is not inherited from the 1874 family.
notes: Two frozen 1201 source-native variants finished. 11731 (native sink_proj + fresh readout) reached test overall=0.5137 fq_acc=0.2540 fq_route=0.2955. 11732 (fresh sink_proj + fresh readout on frozen 1201 core) improved to test overall=0.5238 fq_acc=0.2620 fq_route=0.6197. This is materially better than the fully route-dead qiz ports, so the issue is not that any frozen 1201 head instantly collapses. But both variants remain far below a viable strong-source regime, so the family is retired as noncompetitive rather than promoted or rerun-backed.
close_reason: Retired as a noncompetitive portability negative: source-native frozen-1201 linear heads improved over qiz but still stayed far below viable held-confirm performance.

title: Configure git remote and push experiment commits
notes: User provided remote URL git@github.com:catid/gnn2.git; configuring remote and pushing local commits.
close_reason: Configured origin as git@github.com:catid/gnn2.git and pushed master with the experiment suite, references, report, and verification artifacts.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: audit frozen 1201 view decodability and reader interface
description: Use the strong 1201 ES-assisted source as a frozen audit target. Measure content decodability across the same frozen views that worked on 1874 and 1842, then compare to the failed 1201 selector ports to distinguish weak decodability from reader-parameterization/interface mismatch. Keep routing, memory, and control frozen; do not open a new reader family until the audit says where the mismatch is.
notes: Exact phase10_anchor 1201 audits are now done on base_test full_locked and finalqueryheavy. All three show best probe accuracy 1.0000 with memory_read_state_query as the best representation and sink_state_query baseline_readout_input and final_readout_input all at 1.0000 while packet_state_query stays near 0.24. Combined with qiz route-dead ports this shows the 1201 failure is an interface or initialization mismatch rather than missing source content. Also fixed two regressions in phase10_audit: benchmark-driven model config hydration and query-index conditioning.
close_reason: Exact phase10_anchor 1201 audits showed perfect decodability at sink_state_query baseline_readout_input and final_readout_input across base_test full_locked and finalqueryheavy while qiz reader ports remained route-dead. The issue is interface mismatch rather than missing source content.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: confirm and promote the frozen-1201 12423 interpolation optimum
description: From aj5: local tuning around the 12412 interpolation boundary found a stronger stable point. The 45/55 balanced/routeful blend 12423 reran exactly at overall=0.6344 fq_acc=0.3215 fq_route=0.7674 fq_exit=123.75, which is slightly better than 12412 on overall and route while keeping only a small final-query accuracy giveback. Next step: keep the same frozen 1201 core and same interpolation mechanism, and run confirmation-style evaluation and/or a small promotion panel before doing more local tweaks.
close_reason: phase11_verify confirms on 12423 and 12412 show the interpolation refinement is real on base behavior but does not move the held-confirm frontier. 12423 improves base overall and route over 12412, but full_locked, finalquery_heavy, and longdistance metrics are effectively identical. The issue is therefore closed as a useful mapping result, and the next step is confirmation-aware objective/selector tuning from 12423 rather than more interpolation sweeps.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: scheduled delayed-only source distillation on stable 13411 boundary
description: From gnn2-2rl: delayed-only 13111 source-logit distillation on the stable 13411 readout-only prefix boundary produced a real scout-time gain, and 13612 reran exactly at 0.6650 / 0.3893 / 0.7855 / 124.19. But phase11_verify showed the held-confirm plateau stayed identical to 13411 and confirmed base accuracy slipped slightly. The next narrow question is whether the scout gain only appears because the distillation signal is useful early and harmful late. Keep the same frozen 1201 core and the same 12713/13111 readout-only prefix blend as 13411, keep delayed-only source-logit distillation from 13111, and test schedule-restricted variants such as early-stop, late decay, or short warmstart-only distillation. Do not reopen sink prefixes, do not broaden into other teachers, and do not change routing or memory.
notes: Scaffolded 4 schedule-only delayed-distill variants around the proven 13612 lw=0.10 point: 13711 short warmstart stop@48, 13712 early stop@96, 13713 mid stop@192, and 13714 linear decay 1.0->0.0 over steps 0..192. All keep the same frozen 1201 core, the same 12713/13111 readout-only prefix blend as 13411, the same delayed_only 13111 teacher, and the same proxy full_locked fq-accuracy selector so the comparison isolates schedule only.

title: Phase 9: head-only content shaping after teacher-seeded basin entry
description: Start from the verified phase-8 strong-teacher wait/release-only basin-entry recipe. Freeze the memory/router/control path once the student reaches a teacher-free route-faithful basin, then compare readout-only, sink+readout, and explicit content-focused auxiliary shaping. Goal: recover content without reopening memory and destabilizing the basin.
notes: Phase 9 closeout: Cluster A settled the core split cleanly. Strong direct-entry 1874, medium 1842, and upper-bound 1201 are strongly decodable frozen sources; fragile 1879 is weak-content despite route fidelity. Strict head-only shaping on 1879 is now a fair five-seed negative, while strong-source query-gated finalqweight is the strongest aggregate confirmed head-only family and query-FILM is the strongest alternative-reader family. Medium-source readout-only 9111 also closed as a stable head-only family but remains confirmation-limited. Minimal-safe read-path touches and head-only ES did not beat the best gradient readers. Recommended next step is a single route-regularized read-path adapter on the strongly decodable 1874 source, starting from the best frozen-reader baseline rather than more fragile-source work.
close_reason: Phase 9 complete: strong mapping exit delivered with frozen-state audit, seven full five-seed panels, confirmed strong-source head-only family, confirmed medium-source head-only family, fair fragile-family negative, and gated minimal-safe / head-only ES boundaries.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: Phase 9 pivot tightened: fragile 1879 head-only family remains a fair negative after 9101/9102/9104/9108, with exact rerun 9102 queued. Strong-source alternative-reader branch is the new lead: 9130 verify reached base 0.8115 overall / 0.6334 fq_acc / 0.9482 fq_route / 122.04 exit, and 9142 verify reached 0.8838 / 0.7695 / 0.9385 / 121.31, but both remain confirm-fragile at full_locked ~0.32-0.33 fq_acc with route ~0.877. Launched 9142 rerun + first panel seeds, and launched 9150 query-gated + content-distill to test whether confirm collapse is objective-related rather than missing frozen content.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: Phase 9 mid-campaign update: strong-source query-gated finalqweight family now has a full five-seed panel across 9142/9146/9147/9148/9149. Current means: base 0.8488 overall / 0.7004 fq_acc / 0.9416 fq_route / 121.49 fq_exit; full_locked 0.6534 overall / 0.3237 fq_acc / 0.8771 fq_route / 115.49 fq_exit. So the family is robustly better on base content than older heads, but still confirmation-limited. Fragile-family rerun 9102_rerun1 independently verifies the expected negative boundary: base 0.2488 / 0.2513 / 0.8910 / 122.95, full_locked 0.2498 / 0.2396 / 0.9909 / 127.00, finalquery-heavy 0.2405 / 0.2462 / 0.9845 / 126.98. That confirms the phase-8 story that fragile 1879 retains route but not recoverable content under strict head-only shaping.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: Phase 9 update: Cluster A decodability split is now stable (1874/1842/1201 strongly decodable; 1879 weakly decodable only). Cluster B fragile head-only family is now a fair multi-seed negative: 9102_rerun1 plus 9311-9314 preserve route/exit under locked confirms but stay near-chance on fq_acc. Cluster D content-distill boundaries 9141/9143/9144 are verify-clean overfit cases: strong base content but collapse to ~0.17-0.20 overall on full_locked while keeping route. Cluster E tiny read-path touches 9301-9303 preserve route but do not give a meaningful content lift, so 9341-9344 are only being run as the final fair panel for the best tiny-touch variant. Live promoted work is now the strong-source 9151-9154 query-gated content-distill panel on GPU0 and the tail of Cluster G head-only ES on GPU1; after that GPU1 will take the 9331-9334 query-FILM panel unless G produces a stronger rerun candidate.

comments.author: catid
comments.text: Phase-9 late-state: 9153 landed strong (test 0.8714 / 0.7360 / 0.9332 / 120.82), 9154 still running; fragile E panel has 9341 complete and 9342 landed in the same weak-content route-faithful regime. Important bookkeeping correction: cluster C medium-source head-only coverage is underrepresented in the matrix, so before closeout I am adding the already-run 9135/9136/9145 medium variants to the ledger and running the missing 9111-9114 medium-source head-only scouts plus reruns for 9116 and 9145. Also planning 9202_rerun1 for cluster G and 9301_rerun1 for cluster E so the exact-rerun floor and cluster-specific rerun requirements are satisfied honestly.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: final-query margin shaping on frozen 1874 baseline
description: Start from the same frozen 1874 multiview query-gated baseline used in gnn2-e7s, gnn2-71v, gnn2-zpw, and gnn2-m5v. Do not change the reader, routing, memory, or controller. Stop using confirm-like proxy objectives and instead test direct final-query prediction shaping on the main train split: margin losses, focal-like variants, or other narrow confidence/calibration objectives that act only on final-query-needed examples.
notes: Completed 4-variant final-query shaping sweep on frozen 1874: focal g1/w0.25, focal g2/w0.5, margin 0.2/w0.25, margin 0.4/w0.5. Best variant 11411 reached val overall=0.9917 fq_acc=0.9831 fq_route=0.9474 fq_exit=121.93, still below selector-only 11101. Exact rerun 11411_rerun1 matched exactly, so the family is retired as a reproducible negative.
close_reason: Retired as reproducible negative after fair 4-variant sweep plus exact rerun: direct final-query focal/margin shaping did not beat the selector-only frozen 1874 baseline.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: interpolate balanced and routeful frozen-1201 readers
description: From g51: selector-only changes and light training-time route-preserving regularization both failed on the frozen 1201 probe-init reader. The sharp remaining boundary is now between 11914 (better fq_acc/fq_route, bad overall) and 12011 (better overall, weaker route). Next step: stay on the same frozen 1201 core and test whether parameter interpolation, logit interpolation, or initialization interpolation between the balanced 12011 head and the routeful 11914 head can recover a better tradeoff without unfreezing routing or memory.
close_reason: The 50/50 interpolation between balanced 12011 and routeful 11914 on frozen 1201 produced the first clearly better tradeoff in this branch. Seed12412 reached overall=0.6276 fq_acc=0.3295 fq_route=0.7527 fq_exit=124.26 and reran exactly, beating 11732 11914 and 12011 simultaneously. The family is therefore a rerun-backed positive boundary rather than another negative.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: held-confirm proxy regularizer on frozen 1874 baseline
description: Start from the frozen 1874 multiview query-gated baseline and the negative result from gnn2-71v. Do not add reader changes or memory touches. Test narrow training-time regularizers that explicitly align main-train updates with held-confirm behavior, such as proxy-consistency penalties or light proxy-loss interpolation, while preserving the frozen routing/memory core.
notes: Added low-weight proxy-regularizer sweep configs on frozen 1874 with the best full_locked fq-accuracy selector from gnn2-71v. Planned variants: locked@0.1, locked@0.2, locked+fqh@0.1/0.1, locked+fqh+ld@0.1/0.1/0.1.
close_reason: Completed as a reproducible negative: low-weight proxy-loss interpolation on frozen 1874 improved proxy-fit but did not improve held-confirm final-query accuracy over the selector-only baseline; 11213 confirmed negative and exact rerun matched.
